image: A little clamshell locket clasped tightly shut, something private and personal curled hidden inside, walling itself off from the world.
A steep-thatched cottage seen from the front, its peaked crown and two eaves sloping down to cap and shelter everything beneath it.
A rubber conveyor belt running along the bottom-left, carrying whatever sits on it steadily forward down the road.
A cloth sling curled over from above, its single arm folding down to hug and wrap a hidden bundle tight.
A weathered stone milestone planted at the edge of a rice-paddy grid where dirt roads meet, its carved arrow pointing off to the hamlet.
A polished cowrie shell with a long ridged slit, once traded as coins — treasure and money in one small clam.
A three-tined garden rake gripping the earth, its prongs curled like a grasping hand or a pig's blunt snout.
Three fat water droplets flicked off to the left, mid-splash, scattering down toward a puddle.
A low tuft of wild grass sprouting two blades side by side, a green plant-crown capping whatever grows below.
A wooden ceremonial stake driven slantwise into the ground with a short dart-notch near its head, bound with sacred rope.
A single fletched arrow in mid-flight, feathered nock behind and sharp iron point ahead, streaking dead straight.
An empty cloak slumped and drooping over a chair, folding down like a body gone limp or a flag hung slack.
A rough lean-to awning built against an overhanging cliff face, its slanted roof shading the open shelter beneath.
A carved wooden mask of an old man's face, a long grey beard trailing from its chin — the aged elder's likeness worn on top.
A short broad-bladed hatchet buried in a chopping block, its worn handle still quivering from the last swing.
A woolly ram seen head-on, two curling horns splayed at the top and a fleecy body below, chewing placidly.
A row of four glowing orange coals smoldering along the bottom of a brazier, low licks of heat rising from underneath.
A leather whip snapping down on the right, its lash cracking through the air to rap and drive something into action.
A long-handled measuring dipper hanging on a hook, its deep ladle-bowl scooped and ready to portion out grain.
A tattered black skull-and-crossbones flag whipping on a pole, three ragged streamers trailing off it, screaming must-not-pass.
A plain cloth towel draped over a rail, its two ends hanging straight down, damp and waiting to wipe.
A heavy meteorite that has just plummeted and struck home in a smoking crater, having finally arrived and reached the ground at last.
An open earthen pit dug into a jungle floor, sheer walls on both sides and nothing across the top — a bottomless container waiting to catch whatever falls in.
A curved yew longbow strung taut and vertical, its belly bent into a smooth arc, string humming after a shot.
A plump white grub with a dark head curling and uncurling in the soil, a single wriggling bug you can feel squirming.
A long scarlet chili pepper sharpened to a stinging needle-point, so hot it makes the eyes water — a bitter, prickling barb.
A dumpy old brown sparrow with a comically short stub of a tail, perched fat and grumpy on a wire, too aged to fly far.
Two tiny frozen dew-drops clinging to a black windowpane, hardened into little beads of ice on a bitter winter morning.
A dark cavern gaping open in a wide, exhausted yawn, cold air sighing out of its empty hollow — a void agape and lacking.
Two open palms held side by side and slightly cupped, lifting up an unseen offering from below, ready to catch or present.
A single golden rice stalk heavy with grain, its ripe head drooping over gracefully at the top, swaying in a paddy breeze.
A round dark burrow-hole punched into a grassy bank, a little roofed opening leading down into a hidden underground den.
A wooden bobbin wound with fine silk thread, one loose strand trailing off and dangling in a thin twisting line.
A black stallion at full gallop, mane and four legs flying, kicking up dust as it thunders across an open plain.
A broad single-edged butcher's cleaver, its curved blade catching the light as it hangs from a hook, ready to chop.
A brown hare bounding sharply sideways to duck out of a snare, escaping capture and excusing itself from the trap by a whisker.
A metal toolbox tray lying on its side with its open mouth facing right, three sturdy walls hemming in the tools, one side wide open.
A slick dark eel resting in a lazy S-curve on the riverbed, its tail hooking round in one flowing bend — old and serpentine.
A bristly wild boar with tusks and a curly tail, snorting and rooting in the mud, stubby legs planted wide.
A long springy vaulting pole planted mid-run and bending forward, carrying an unseen athlete in one enormous long stride.
A three-fingered arcade grabber-claw hanging at the left edge, fingers half-clenched and lowering to snatch a prize.
A carved seal-stamp pressed down into a blob of red wax, leaving a crisp imprint — a small kneeling stamp bowing to make its mark.
A wooden hand-plow with a curved handle and a bright blade biting into dark furrows, dragged forward through the field.
A single bare, weathered rib-bone lying in the dust, picked clean and cracked — a splinter of death left behind.
A narrow sickbed tilted against the wall, its blanket rumpled and a fever-cloth draped over the rail, waiting for a patient.
A pair of snowshoes planted toes-outward in fresh powder, their webbed frames spread wide like two footprints climbing apart.
A rough grey boulder wedged under a cliff-overhang, squat and immovable, moss creeping across its cold flank.
A small stone roadside altar-shrine on the left of the path, an offering bowl smoking with incense, a spirit hovering above it.
Two tall green bamboo stalks side by side, their drooping leaf-tufts hanging over like paired brushstrokes, creaking in the wind.
A cloth banner stamped with a single bold clan crest, snapping proudly from a pole above a household gate.
Two matching white flight-feathers laid side by side, each barbed vane curving up — a bird's wings shed onto the ground.
A cobra wound into a tight comma-shaped coil, its head tucked into the curl, ready to spring.
A wide horizontal fishing net stretched flat with a mesh grid, cast down over the water to trap whatever swims below.
A striped level-crossing barrier already lowered flat across the road, halting everything — the gate has closed, it is done.
A stubby wooden peg jammed into a hole in the wall, its short blunt arm jutting out to hold up whatever is slung over it.
A pale marble bust on a plinth — just the head and neck of a stern figure, chin lifted, eyes staring straight ahead.
A tall halberd stood upright, its barbed spear-point gleaming at the top and a tassel swinging from the shaft.
A flat-topped desert butte standing utterly still against the sky, rooted and unmoving, refusing to budge for the passing wind.
A stepped green hill of stacked earth-terraces rising on the right edge, a footpath zigzagging up its ledges toward a village.
A fat rolled bolt of cloth unspooling a long strip of fabric down onto the shop counter, its loose end trailing like a foot.
A big pale conch shell propped upright, its spiral crown at the top and two little feet at the base — held up like a printed page.
A bare cliff overhang jutting out over empty space, a slab of stone roofing a shallow hollow beneath it.
